title: Reddit Optimization
content_md:
# Reddit Optimization

- Code: f74717d6
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=f74717d689404852b06cded8fdd73d40

transcript.text: I've mentioned Reddit several times and how important Reddit is to ChatGPT.
So let's talk about how to optimize for Reddit.
Reddit is basically the front door, okay?
Why is everything bold?
Reddit is by far the most cited community source in ChatGPT answers, okay?
And the threads in Reddit will often show up verbatim in ChatGPT answers.
There's a famous example of somebody asking ChatGPT how to prevent cheese from sliding
off of pizza and ChatGPT said that they should glue it on.
That is a direct quote from a Reddit post which obviously was a ingest and ChatGPT didn't
get the joke, okay?
And this is kind of funny, right?
ChatGPT is going to reference the user's natural questions to simulate human consensus, okay?
So what that means is it's just going to grab information from Reddit and repeat it because
it knows that if somebody on Reddit said it, if ChatGPT repeats that, it's going to sound
more human, which, you know, the AI wants to sound human.
So one of the reasons, and Google also is obsessed with Reddit these days, Reddit is real people.
Those real people are giving uncensored, anonymous opinions.
That means that those opinions are seen as high trust, according to ChatGPT and Google.
In addition to that, metrics like upvotes can signal trust.
The citations and mentions on Reddit will last for years, okay?
And most cities have local subreddits, especially larger cities.
Now, there are other platforms, other sources.
Reddit's the big one, but we also have Quora, Nextdoor, Facebook groups, comment sections,
and niche forms.
There's a couple of examples here that you could use.
And again, you're not trying to get links back to your website, right?
We're not using Google here, so we don't need links.
We just need the business name mentioned, okay?
So now, when you're doing this on Reddit, I'm trying to put my face somewhere, when
you're doing this on Reddit, just create a legitimate account, okay?
And with that account, work to build karma, work to build history before you start posting
about your business.
Engage in existing threads, reply naturally, like how you would reply on Reddit about non-competitive
businesses first, right?
So don't just jump in and start talking about roofers if you're a roofer, just jump in and
start talking about things in Dallas, restaurants, places, things you like to do, anything that's
helpful to people.
And of course, mirror real language, basically, don't be spammy, right?
Okay, now, you should strongly consider including the business name in your bio.
This sounds crazy, but if you include the business name in your bio, ChatGPT will be
able to link your Reddit username to your business so that things you say on Reddit
with your username, ChatGPT can use as a signal of trust and authority, right?
Another good idea, if somebody has an issue on a subreddit, I run a planning company in
Dallas, this is a common issue in Tempstrop, suddenly, then talk about how to fix it.
ChatGPT will see this, it will remember it, and it will reference this, if someone else
in Dallas asks about this exact issue, it will recommend you to fix it, okay?
Very, very powerful that ChatGPT can link the business name in your bio to your Reddit
username.
Another good example is this branded AMA or value thread.
So if you're a roofer in Dallas, you can post, hey, I own a roofing company, what is the
question you have about roofs?
You can do this in the homeowners, the city subreddits, the DIY subreddit, home improvement
subreddit, anything that to provide value, don't create an account and then go do this
straight away.
This is something you would do after you've already built some trust with your account,
okay?
Now, to help find some threads that you can engage with, Google search, obviously you're
going to do the site colon Reddit so that it only finds Reddit stuff, and then best
service in city.
You can also ask ChatGPT, what do Reddit users say about me?
Basically the key, right, I cannot say this often enough, I think I said it five times,
don't be spammy, just try to engage naturally, you're just trying to provide value.
Not for Reddit users, though if you provide value for Reddit users, that's awesome.
You're trying to provide value in such a way that ChatGPT recommends you as an authority
and more importantly, it recommends your business as an authority because your business
is the one posting, adding value, answering questions, that's the big thing, that's how
to utilize Reddit to get ChatGPT to recommend your business to more ChatGPT searchers.
